one_time_description: Agricultural Benefits Specific Claim settlement from Canada under Treaty 8 - $136,431,042 received, with $86,204,219 in related disbursements (net surplus of $50,226,823)
economic_notes: Four government business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method: Sucker Creek Holdings Ltd., Sucker Creek Gas Utility, Sucker Creek First Nation Co-op, SCFN Services Limited Partnership. Received $4,162,665 in additional COVID-19 funding from ISC.
notes: Major one-time item: $136,431,042 Agricultural Benefits Specific Claim settlement under Treaty 8, with $86,204,219 in related disbursements (per capita distributions and costs), resulting in net $50,226,823 added to accumulated surplus. A trust (Sucker Creek First Nation Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust) was created and fully consolidated; the Nation's equity in the trust is $85,250,720. An RBC bridge loan of $71,151,718 was taken to fund PCD payments before settlement proceeds were received; net balance at year end was $35,023,897 (Agricultural Benefits Settlement Loan). Prior period restatement for SCFN Services LP investment decreased opening accumulated surplus by $286,185. No schedule of remuneration provided in the document. OSR calculation includes Agricultural Benefits Settlement revenue and SCFN entity losses which are non-recurring. total_government_transfers includes ISC $18,873,846 + LSLIRC $1,163,484 + Government of Alberta $578,562 + CMHC $606,915 = $21,222,807 (FNDF $390,793 added = $21,613,600; Ottawa Trust Funds $67,781 added = $21,681,381; LSLIRC advance excluded as debt). Restricted cash of $2,733,293 included within total cash of $14,724,802.
